rationale.R1.1: A broad request for judgment centrally invites assessment of whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent and compelling.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact requires checking whether the motivation, method, results, and conclusion form a consistent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact reports empirical gains and human evaluation, judging it centrally includes whether the stated claims are supported by the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes first-work and contribution-delta claims, so an overall opinion centrally includes assessing novelty at a high level.
rationale.RX.1: Any judgment should disclose uncertainty, especially because only the artifact text is available and no external verification is requested.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must account for avoiding endorsement of claims stronger than the reported evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain within the requested evaluative scope and not execute edits, rewrite the artifact, or perform an external audit.
rationale.RX.4: The judgment should avoid encouraging misleading deployment claims or unsupported conclusions in ambiguity-sensitive settings.
rationale.RX.5: The assessment should remain traceable to statements present in the provided artifact rather than unstated assumptions.
